_metadata.description: Obstetrics framework — pregnancy physiology, prenatal/intrapartum/postpartum complications.

cardiovascular: BP drops mid-trimester, rises near term

first_trimester: labs: blood type, Rh, CBC, urine, STI, HIV, rubella
first trimester aneuploidy screen (NT + PAPP-A + hCG)

first_trimester.ectopic_pregnancy: abnormal hCG rise + sonogram with no IUP; RUPTURE = emergency
first_trimester.molar_pregnancy: very high hCG, snowstorm on US

preeclampsia.criteria: new HTN ≥140/90 after 20 wk + proteinuria OR end-organ damage
preeclampsia.severe: BP ≥160/110, AKI, hepatic, neuro symptoms, thrombocytopenia
preeclampsia.treatment: delivery curative; magnesium for seizure prophylaxis

third_trimester.HELLP_syndrome: Hemolysis, Elevated Liver enzymes, Low Platelets
third_trimester.placenta_previa: placenta over cervical os → painless 3rd trimester bleeding
third_trimester.placental_abruption: premature separation → painful bleeding + tetanic uterus
third_trimester.vasa_previa: fetal vessels over os → rupture with membranes

intrapartum.chorioamnionitis: maternal fever + uterine tenderness + fetal tachy
intrapartum.uterine_rupture: severe pain + fetal distress + loss of contraction pattern
intrapartum.amniotic_fluid_embolism: sudden cardiovascular collapse + DIC during/after delivery

postpartum.mastitis: breast pain + erythema + fever in breastfeeding (S. aureus)
postpartum.postpartum_DVT_PE: highest VTE risk in first 6 weeks postpartum
postpartum.postpartum_depression: 10-15% incidence, beyond 'baby blues' (which is normal first 2 wk)

fetal_assessment.biophysical_profile_BPP: tone, movement, breathing, fluid, NST (10 max)
